rationale: KubeAI ollamaStartupProbeScript constructs shell command via fmt.Sprintf on unsanitized model URL — classic CWE-78 OS command injection in AI inference operator. AIDEFEND covers via Harden-tactic input-sanitization techniques; nearest_should_include any AID-H-* harden technique.

MCP-server-kubernetes port_forward tool argument injection via space-splitting (CWE-88). MCP-specific tooling pattern: AIDEFEND lacks MCP-protocol-specific hardening guidance (this is genuinely novel post-MCP-spec attack class). System maps to AID-M-009 by lexical overlap, but the right answer is MCP-tool-call validation guidance, which AIDEFEND doesn't have yet. GAP.

Mesop WebSocket unbounded thread creation → DoS (CWE-125+CWE-400). AI app framework. AIDEFEND covers DoS hardening via AID-H techniques (rate-limiting, resource quotas) and AID-D-* (anomaly detection on connection rates).

sagemaker-python-sdk capture_dependencies CWE-78 OS command injection via requirements_path. Pure AI/ML library. AID-M-009 (model supply chain integrity) is a reasonable nearest match; AID-H-* harden techniques also apply. Already-covered.

Rasa malicious-model load → RCE (CWE-502+CWE-94). Classic AI supply-chain pickle attack. AIDEFEND directly covers via AID-M-009 (Model Supply Chain Integrity) and AID-H-* (deserialization hardening / safetensors).
CWE-502 unsafe deserialization in AI/ML library; AID-M-009 (model supply chain integrity) is the right home. Bridge correctly suggests harden+isolate from CWE-502.

CWE-502 deserialization in AI/ML stack. Nearest AID-M-002.005 maps to model robustness — slightly off; better fit is AID-M-009 (supply chain) but the bridge rationale (harden+isolate from CWE-502) gives reviewer the right tactic family.

Doccano (AI annotation tool) privilege escalation via model_attribs — CWE-918 SSRF flag suggests URL-fetching path. AIDEFEND covers via AID-H-* (URL allowlists, harden boundaries) plus AID-I-* isolation. Bridge correctly hints harden+isolate.
Doccano CWE-94 code injection. AID-H-019.* is input-handling harden — correct family. Bridge contributes harden+isolate from CWE-94 (code-eval boundaries).
CWE-78 command injection in AI/ML dependency. AID-M-009 is reasonable for the supply-chain framing; AID-H-* harden also applies. Coverage exists.
CWE-287 improper authentication on AI/ML service. AIDEFEND covers via AID-H-* (auth boundary hardening) and AID-M-009 (supply chain). The ML-context bridge hint is appropriate.

CWE-20+CWE-400+CWE-915 cluster on AI/ML library. Combination of input validation + resource exhaustion + dynamic-attribute pollution. AIDEFEND covers each via Harden tactic; CWE-915 bridge hint adds attribute-allowlist guidance.

WordPress 'My Calendar' plugin IDOR (CWE-639). Pure web-app vulnerability; not AI-related. AIDEFEND should not claim mapping here — the BM25 high score (163) is over-confident lexical overlap. GENUINE GAP in scope-determination logic; system should reject as out_of_scope.
Weblate (translation platform) privilege issue (CWE-269). Not AI-defensive; out-of-scope for AIDEFEND. Should be rejected with reason=out_of_scope, not mapped to any AID-*.

Prometheus (often deployed alongside AI inference for observability) auth-related issue (CWE-303). AID-M-009 supply-chain coverage is borderline-relevant; could plausibly be Shape-A defendsAgainst extension. Marking covered conservatively.

Kyverno (K8s policy engine) CWE-200 information exposure + CWE-522 cred storage. K8s often hosts AI workloads; secrets exposure relevant to AID-M-009 supply chain + AID-I-* isolate. AIDEFEND covers via these.

CairoSVG DoS via crafted SVG (CWE-400). General library DoS, possibly used in AI doc-ingest pipelines but not AI-defensive in the AIDEFEND sense. BM25 score 101 is over-confident; should reject as out_of_scope unless reviewer confirms AI-pipeline relevance.

Krayin Laravel CRM CWE-918 SSRF. Not AI-defensive directly, but the AID-I-002.* (isolation / SSRF egress controls) is the correct *defensive pattern* — AIDEFEND defenses extend to general application security. Reasonable Shape-A extension candidate if reviewer chooses.
Random Go shopping-cart library command injection (CWE-77). No AI relevance. Out_of_scope for AIDEFEND; system mapped to AID-H-031.002 by lexical overlap with 'command' tokens — false-positive.
Weblate path-traversal + info-exposure (CWE-22+CWE-200). Not AI. Out_of_scope; current BM25 mapping to AID-H-009.002 is a lexical false-positive.

Erlang Ash framework unbounded resource (CWE-400+CWE-770). Used in some AI app backends. AIDEFEND DoS-hardening covers; bridge contributes harden+detect from CWE-770. Borderline AI-relevant — mark covered.
Keycloak missing-auth (CWE-306). Keycloak commonly fronts AI service auth. AID-H-004.001 (auth boundary hardening) is exactly right; bridge confirms harden+default-deny.
Keycloak SAML adapter improper authn (CWE-1287). AIDEFEND AID-D-001.* (detect adversarial auth) is reasonable; this is detection-tactic territory. Coverage exists.
AI/ML library CWE-20 input validation + nearest AID-E-004 (Evict — incident response after compromise). Reasonable mapping; combined with bridge harden+detect hints from CWE-20.
AI/ML library path-traversal cluster (CWE-22+CWE-61). Bridge correctly maps CWE-22 to harden+isolate. Supply-chain framing via AID-M-009 fits.
